revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]]

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a <- for page at newadvent.org | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} Example using {{para|url}} (could point to any source of the page): *{{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} {{Cite CE1913 | url = http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Catholic_Encyclopedia_%281913%29/Ecclesiastical_Heraldry | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry <- for the Catholic Encyclopedia article name | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Cite_Catholic_Encyclopedia%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{cs1 wrapper|base=Cite encyclopedia}} This template may be used to cite the 1913 ''[[:en:Catholic Encyclopedia|Catholic Encyclopedia]]''. The text is available either at [http://www.newadvent.org/cathen/index.html NewAdvent.org] or at [[wikisource:Catholic Encyclopedia (1913)]], and this template allows either source to be referenced. ===Usage=== Full template with available options is shown below. If {{para|wstitle}} is set, {{para|title}} and {{para|url}} are ignored. If {{para|wstitle}} or {{para|url}} are set, {{para|id}} is ignored. {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = | id = | url = | title = | author = | first = | last = | author-link = | access-date = | page = | pages = | quote = | ref = | short = | noicon = }} ===Examples=== Example using {{para|wstitle}} (wikisource): *{{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | author = A. C. Fox-Davies | first = | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry <- for page at wikisource | author = A. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author }} Example using {{para|id}} (newadvent.org): *{{Cite CE1913 | id = 07243a | title = Ecclesiastical heraldry | author = A. C. C. Fox-Davies <- optional | first = <- alternate form for author | last = | author-link = Arthur Charles Fox-Davies <- optional, presumes author | access-date = 2006-09-01 }} There is also a short form which might be appear in a "Notes" section. This should only be used if the full form is given in a different section. To work parameter short= can be set to one of more alphanumeric characters. For example: short=yes, short=x or short=1. An Example using {{para|wstitle}}: * {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} {{Cite CE1913 | wstitle = Ecclesiastical Heraldry | short = yes }} The {{para|noicon}} option is useful when the citation appears in a Wikisource-inline list and {{para|wstitle}} is being used (and like the parameter short it set with one of more alphanumeric characters). For example: * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} * {{Wikisource-inline|list= ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Abel (1)|noicon=x}} ** {{Cite CE1913|wstitle=Cain|noicon=1|short=1}} }} ===Hidden categories=== This template includes a hidden category as several hidden sub categories: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Contained within this category is a subcategory populated by {{tl|CE1913}} it is called: * [[:Category:Articles incorporating text from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia]] Articles that fail to include the required {{para|title}} or {{para|wstitle}} will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with no article parameter}} Articles that use an unnamed parameter will be placed in: * {{clc|Articles incorporating a citation from the 1913 Catholic Encyclopedia with an unnamed parameter}} ===See also=== * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia poster}} can be placed in the external links section when a ''Catholic Encyclopedia'' article has information relevant to the Wikipedia article but is not cited in the article. * {{Tl|Catholic Encyclopedia}}—Similar functionality to this template, but precedes the citation with an attribution message (see [[WP:PLAGIARISM]]) * {{Tl|Cite encyclopedia}}—The standard citation template called by this specialised citation template. {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Catholic Encyclopedia templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Specific-source templates|Catholic Encyclopedia]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
